key_insight: Machine-learning Monte Carlo/molecular dynamics reveals that Al content controls whether (MoCrTi)$_{100-x}$Al$_x$ refractory HEAs undergo one or two ordering transitions, with low-temperature B2-like chemical ordering strongly reshaping structure and stiffness.
engineering_relevance: Chemical ordering can be used as a design lever to tune elastic constants and moduli in refractory high-entropy alloys, enabling higher stiffness at selected compositions rather than a simple monotonic trend with Al content.
method: Hybrid Monte Carlo + molecular dynamics simulations
Temperature-dependent thermodynamics, short-range order, and elastic-property analysis
evidence_signal: The abstract reports heat capacities and short-range order parameters showing distinct order-disorder transitions, plus composition-dependent elastic constants and moduli that peak at Mo30Cr30Ti30Al10.
watch_item: Predictions depend on the accuracy and transferability of the learned interatomic potential, so experimental validation is important before using the trends for alloy screening.
paper_title: Study of ordering in (MoCrTi)$_{100-x}$Al$_x$ refractory high-entropy alloys using machine learning interatomic potential
